name: Cam's Kiosk
cuisine: Cafe by day, Italian wine bar by night
description: A relaxed cafe inside a wing of the Abbotsford Convent, serving coffee, soups, sandwiches and seasonal salads by day. By night it turns into a sleek wine bar with Italian-style plates and pasta.

name: Urbanstead
description: A minimum-waste bakery and community food space run by bakers Michael and Pippa James in a converted Abbotsford warehouse. Known for sourdough, morning buns and whole-food baking, it also runs hands-on baking classes.

name: Andrew's Hamburgers
description: A long-running Melbourne burger institution serving classic old-school Aussie hamburgers with the lot. One of the city's oldest burger joints, it's a no-frills Albert Park favourite.

name: Piccolo Vicolo
description: A humble Italian cafe serving traditional home-style cooking for breakfast and lunch, with dinner on Thursdays and Fridays. It's best known for its meatball sandwich.

name: The Mazu Cafe
description: A Balwyn brunch cafe where the chef's Taiwanese background flavours an all-day menu, with standouts like shokupan toast, popcorn chicken and braised pork rice. There's a leafy courtyard out the back for weekend dining.

name: Chaozhou Malaya Bistro Cafe
description: An intimate Balwyn North bistro serving home-style Teochew (Chaozhou) and Malaysian Chinese dishes. It's known for its crisp-edged oyster omelette, bak kut teh and char kway teow, so booking ahead is wise.

name: Hong's Dumplings
description: A small, busy Shanghainese eatery in Box Hill South turning out handmade dumplings and soup noodles. The signature pan-fried pork buns (sheng jian bao) come filled with a burst of hot broth.

name: Chooka's
description: A tiny, charming Japanese cafe in Brunswick known for its soulful omusubi (rice balls) and matcha drinks, alongside teishoku set meals and rice bowls. With only a handful of tables, queues are common.

name: Cumbé
description: A Brunswick sandwich shop serving Mexico City-style tortas, led by the torta de chilaquiles, plus Mexican-spiced takes on Aussie pies and rolls. Specialty coffee comes from local roasters Market Lane and Seven Seeds.

name: Good Boys Deli
description: Good Boys Deli is a Brunswick sandwich shop slinging generously filled focaccias and rolls, with standouts like wagyu pastrami. It also pours coffee for a quick weekday lunch or weekend bite.

name: Lunar by Hikari
description: Lunar by Hikari is a spacious Japanese-style cafe specialising in pour-over coffee from a rotating list of Japanese roasters. It pairs the brews with Japanese-inflected sweets and a full menu of savoury breakfast and lunch dishes.

name: Alex Bake 3057
description: Alex Bake 3057 is an artisan French bakery and cafe in Brunswick East known for its sourdough loaves and flaky viennoiserie. Expect croissants, fruit tarts and other freshly baked pastries alongside coffee.

name: Charrd
description: Charrd is a tightly focused burger shop serving just two halal smash burgers, fries and charcoal-grilled wings. Its signature burger, loaded with truffle aioli, chilli jam and caramelised onions, earned it a spot among the world's best burgers.

name: Daphne
description: Daphne is an all-day public house and diner from the Etta team, with a walk-in front bar and a table-service dining room out back. The menu centres on a custom wood-fire grill and oven, always featuring pasta, a burger and a steak.

name: Old Palm Liquor
description: Old Palm Liquor is a wood-fired restaurant and natural wine bar centred on an open parilla grill, with fire-licked, South African-inspired share plates. It pairs the food with an extensive list of around 20 wines by the glass and over 1,000 bottles in a retro, plant-filled diner setting.

name: Sani
description: Sani is an all-day cafe set inside a restored Victorian-era bank, serving Locale Coffee Roasters espresso and single-origin pour-overs. Its globally inspired brunch menu spans Turkish and Japanese-style egg dishes, loaded breakfast rolls and vibrant lunch plates.

name: A Little Joy
description: A Little Joy is a casual Shanghai-style eatery known for its pan-fried buns in pork, vegetable and prawn, plus xiao long bao soup dumplings, wontons and Shanghai noodles. It also makes its own soy milk and offers takeaway.

name: Attakai Garden
description: Attakai Garden is a compact Japanese matcha and specialty coffee window tucked inside Collins Arcade. It serves high-quality matcha drinks, espresso and matcha-based sweet treats as a calm pause from the city.

name: Aung Lo
description: Aung Lo is a casual laneway diner serving mookata, the Thai barbecue-and-hotpot hybrid where meats are grilled over charcoal while broth simmers below. It draws on Korean, Japanese and Chinese flavours, with premium cuts like A5 wagyu and fruity bingsu for dessert.

name: BKK
description: BKK is a small, lively Thai barbecue canteen with an open charcoal kitchen putting diners front-row to the flames. It serves Bangkok-inspired street food spanning snacks, grilled selections and wok specials drawn from across Thailand.

name: Bakemono Bakers
description: Bakemono Bakers is a Japanese-inspired bakery known for French-Japanese pastries, including a yuzu almond croissant, canelés and shokupan loaves. Its perpetually sold-out garlic bread and small batches mean queues form before opening.

name: Bing Chillin
description: Bing Chillin is a dessert shop specialising in bingsu, the Korean shaved-ice treat with a silky milk-snow texture. It offers creative Asian-inspired flavours like biscoff, matcha, ube and hojicha, alongside signature waffles and drinks.

name: Bloomwood Coffee & Food
description: Bloomwood is a cafe and bakehouse focused on artisan viennoiserie that blends French technique with Asian flavours, from pain au chocolat to corn cheese and taro danishes. It also serves house-baked open focaccia and specialty coffee.

name: Boon Choou
description: Boon Choou is a Chinatown-laneway restaurant bringing four regional Thai cuisines together under one roof: central Bangkok-style, northern Lanna, north-eastern Isan and southern Pattani. The expansive menu is built around the team's family recipes and home-style cooking.

name: Bowltiful Halal Lanzhou Beef Noodle
description: Bowltiful specialises in halal Lanzhou-style hand-pulled beef noodles, with diners choosing their preferred noodle thickness for bowls of spicy beef noodle soup and braised brisket. It also serves dry and stir-fried noodles, spicy skewers and house teas.

name: Butchers Diner
description: Butchers Diner is a casual late-night diner set in a former butcher's boning room, fitted out with retro tiles, counter seating and 70s-style lighting. The menu centres on burgers, rolls, grills and roasts with a focus on quality meat.

name: Caffe E Torta
description: Caffe E Torta is a long-running family-owned Italian cafe tucked inside the historic Royal Arcade. It is known for its coffee, baguettes and house-made cakes alongside hot dishes like a slow-cooked beef ragu tagliatelle.

name: Caretaker's Cottage
description: Caretaker's Cottage is a tiny, walk-in-only cocktail bar set in a former caretaker's cottage behind Wesley Church, and one of the world's most acclaimed bars. It is famed for freezer-cold martinis poured with custom gin, a Guinness boilermaker and a monthly-changing list of signature cocktails.

name: Common Cuts
description: Common Cuts is a craft-led grill house and vinyl listening bar serving fire-grilled steak in a range of cuts and steak frites. It pairs its meat-focused menu with playful cocktails and snacks like oysters and grilled scallops.

name: Commonplace Coffee Brewers
description: Commonplace Coffee Brewers is a specialty coffee cafe pulling shots on a La Marzocco Strada and serving Toby's Estate beans across espresso, batch brew and V60 filter. It's a welcoming spot popular with city locals and corporate workers.

name: Coppe Pan Japanese Bakery
description: Coppe Pan is a Japanese bakery making everything by hand, known for filled koppepan rolls in both savoury and sweet styles. Options range from chicken katsu and curry omelette rolls to matcha cream and peanut butter sandwiches.

name: Crystal Jade Restaurant
description: Crystal Jade is a modern Cantonese restaurant in Chinatown known for all-day yum cha and dim sum made fresh on site, ordered by menu sheet rather than trolley. Its signature dish is wok-fried snow crab with foie gras.

name: Dao Noodle
description: Dao Noodle specialises in handmade dao xiao mian, knife-shaved noodles cut straight into boiling water in the style of China's Shanxi province. The menu also features homemade dumplings, rou jia mo burgers and cold sides.

name: Disuko
description: Disuko is a vinyl-led Japanese rooftop bar and restaurant in the CBD, serving izakaya bites, nigiri and sushi alongside DJ sets under disco lights. It offers a la carte and set menus in a moody, Tokyo-nightlife setting.

name: Dohtonbori
description: Dohtonbori is a Japanese restaurant specialising in DIY okonomiyaki and teppanyaki cooked at your own table hotplate. It delivers a fun, interactive dining experience across its Melbourne locations.

name: Doju
description: Doju is a modern Korean restaurant in the CBD built around shared plates made with seasonal local produce and thoughtful Korean condiments. It has earned a Chef's Hat and is known for bold, refined flavours.

name: Dongdaemoon
description: Dong Dae Moon is a Korean comfort-food spot inspired by Seoul's Dongdaemun market, with fast counter service. It is known for gimbap and everyday K-food served for lunch and dinner.

name: EARL Canteen
description: EARL Canteen is a daytime canteen known for some of Melbourne's favourite made-to-order sandwiches, plus salads, breakfast and coffee using locally sourced produce. It is a popular CBD lunch spot.

name: EatAlley
description: A Malaysian hawker-style food hall serving classics from ten iconic stalls cooked in one kitchen, with ordering done via QR code. Known for Hainanese chicken rice, laksa, braised duck noodles and KL Hokkien mee.
address: Shop 11, Level 2, QV Melbourne, Swanston St, Melbourne

name: Enelssie Cafe & Grill
description: A Filipino cafe and grill serving classic, family-recipe dishes in a bright space filled with cycling memorabilia. Known for crispy pork bagnet, grilled chicken inasal, sinigang and desserts like halo-halo and ube cake.

name: FOODAO
description: A casual Chinese eatery specialising in xian bing, pan-fried stuffed hand pies with fillings like black pepper beef and spicy Korean chicken, alongside dumplings and fluffy steamed buns. Located in the Melbourne Central food court.
address: Shop LG02, 211 La Trobe St, Melbourne

name: Fishbowl
description: A fast-casual chain serving build-your-own poke bowls with fresh Australian tuna and salmon, plus vegetarian and plant-based options. Known for the OG salmon bowl and a healthy, quick alternative to typical takeaway.

name: Flower Drum
description: Flower Drum is a Melbourne institution offering refined Cantonese fine dining in an opulent Chinatown dining room. It is renowned for its Peking duck, mud crab and produce-led sharing plates.

name: Food Hall
description: A compact, casual food court running five stalls under one roof: Thai, Malaysian, Korean, Japanese and Italian. Open around the clock for everything from nasi lemak and Korean fried chicken to ramen and pasta.

name: French Fix
description: French Fix is a small CBD cafe serving authentic French baguette sandwiches with classic fillings, alongside croissants, pastries and coffee. The Le Roast-Beef baguette is a daily favourite that regularly sells out.

name: Fuwa Kakigori
description: Fuwa Kakigori specialises in kakigori, Japanese shaved ice hand-shaped into photogenic domes and dressed with syrups, condensed milk, fruit, matcha, mochi and meringue. It also offers DIY daifuku sets and drinks like strawberry matcha and hojicha lattes.

name: Gimbap Jang Go Mama
description: Jang Go Mama is a tiny Korean spot known for some of the best gimbap in the city, with fillings like spicy fishcake, beef and kimchi. The compact menu also includes naengmyeon, cheesy tteokbokki, ramyun and dumpling soup.

name: Göz City
description: Goz City is a family-run Turkish eatery devoted to gozleme, the hand-rolled flatbread cooked to order on a hot plate. Core fillings include cheese and spinach, minced meat, herbed chicken and mushroom and vegetables, with pide, borek and salads rounding out the menu.

name: Hansik
description: Hansik is a Korean restaurant and bar in the CBD with a modern, social atmosphere. The menu spans sizzling grilled meats, comforting stews and a range of flavour-packed small plates.

name: Heng (Heng Thai Local Food Hall)
description: Heng is a sprawling 120-seat Thai food hall housing several kitchens under one roof, including a boat-noodle specialist, an Isaan street-food counter and an all-you-can-eat mookata BBQ. The decor channels a nostalgic 1980s-90s Thai home, and it is best known for boat noodles and Thai BBQ.

name: Heyday Hong Kong Cafe
description: Heyday is a Chinatown cha chaan teng serving traditional Hong Kong cafe fare, blending Cantonese cooking with the Western-Chinese hybrid dishes typical of Hong Kong diners. It is a long-running, no-frills spot popular for an authentic, everyday Hong Kong menu.

name: Ho Jiak Melbourne
description: Ho Jiak is chef Junda Khoo's ambitious Malaysian destination spread across three storeys, comprising hawker-style takeaway, a beer hall and an upstairs dining room. It is known for playful, contemporary Malaysian cooking with signatures like laksa bombs, roast duck and char kway teow topped with mud crab.

name: Hua Lu Peking Duck
description: Hua Lu specialises in crispy, carved-to-order Peking duck alongside a broader northern-leaning Chinese menu. It is a popular CBD destination for the duck and traditional Chinese dining.

name: Ipoh Town
description: Ipoh Town serves traditional Malaysian hawker dishes inspired by the street-food stalls of Ipoh, sticking to authentic recipes and techniques. It is known for an everyday hawker-style menu of Malaysian favourites.

name: Jalan Alor Melbourne
description: Jalan Alor recreates Kuala Lumpur's famous food-street experience with affordable, casual Malaysian hawker fare. It's known for char kway teow, beef rendang, Sarawak laksa and satay skewers.

name: Jin Noodle Restaurant
description: Jin Noodle is a specialist Shanxi knife-sliced noodle restaurant where the noodles are cut fresh at the counter. It's known for its rich braised beef noodle soup with chewy, bouncy noodles.

name: Jom
description: Jom is a casual, halal-friendly Malaysian eatery serving affordable comfort-food classics. It's the sister venue to Jom Corner.

name: Jom Corner
description: Jom Corner is a small kopitiam-style cafe serving Kuala Lumpur and Klang Valley street food. It's known for its chicken rice with house chilli oil, kaya toast, curry puffs and teh tarik.

name: Kata Kita
description: Kata Kita is a modern Indonesian restaurant focused on the flavours of Bali, pairing traditional recipes with local ingredients in shared plates. It's known for customisable nasi campur and desserts like pandan Basque cheesecake.

name: KatsuRetsu Co
description: An express tonkatsu joint serving Japanese-style crumbed pork cutlets coated in fresh nama panko and deep-fried, alongside fried prawns and oysters. Cutlets come with shredded cabbage and a choice of sauces including tartare, Japanese curry and Nagoya-style dark miso.

name: Khao Man Gai
description: A casual Thai eatery dedicated to khao man gai, fragrant jasmine rice cooked in poached-chicken broth and served with sliced chicken. Around nine variations are offered, from skin-on poached chicken to fried, plus chicken curries and noodle soup.

name: Kikanbo Melbourne
description: The Australian outpost of Tokyo's famous Kikanbo, specialising in fiery karashibi miso ramen built on a rich pork-and-chicken broth with chashu pork. Diners pick their own levels of chilli heat (kara) and numbing sansho pepper (shibi), from zero up to demon-strength.

name: Kombu
description: A cosy Japanese spot at Queen Victoria Market specialising in teishoku set meals and chazuke (rice and protein with poured-over broth), with proteins like mackerel, salmon and miso-marinated chicken. Also known for its coffee and matcha drinks, including a cult pistachio matcha.

name: Korchi City
description: A great-value Korean-Chinese restaurant known for jjajangmyeon, handmade noodles in thick black bean sauce with pork and vegetables, and jjamppong, a spicy seafood noodle soup. Free kimchi and pickled daikon are on offer.

name: Lang Baan
description: Lang Baan is a bright, colourful Thai restaurant serving dishes from across Thailand, from pad see ew and crispy pork belly to red duck curry and sharing-sized pad kaprao. It's also known for Thai milk tea bingsu and cendol-style sweet drinks.

name: Lee Ho Fook
description: Lee Ho Fook is a two-hatted modern Chinese restaurant by chef Victor Liong, reimagining Chinese cooking through a refined Australian lens. It offers a la carte and tasting menus, with a casual downstairs chef's counter and a more formal upstairs dining room, and is known for dishes like its Peking duck and crispy eggplant.

name: Little Ramen Bar
description: Little Ramen Bar is a compact, unpretentious ramen spot serving rich, carefully made broths from classic tonkotsu to spicy miso and shoyu, with chewy noodles and traditional toppings. Vegetarian and vegan ramen options are also available.

name: Liyin Rice Roll Master
description: Liyin Rice Roll Master is a budget-friendly Cantonese eatery devoted almost entirely to cheung fun (rice noodle rolls) and congee. The rolls are made from scratch with rice stone-milled into batter daily and steamed to order, available plain or with more than 20 fillings from prawn to beef and pork.

name: Lulu's Char Koay Teow
description: Lulu's Char Koay Teow is a Malaysian hawker-style spot widely rated among the best in town for its signature wok-fried char koay teow. The noodles are tossed in pork lard with Chinese sausage, egg, bean sprouts, chives and chilli, and served seven ways including with prawns, duck egg, cockles, squid and razor clams.

name: Maison Bâtard
description: Maison Bâtard is a sprawling four-level French dining destination spanning a restaurant with an oyster and caviar bar, an indoor-outdoor rooftop terrace and a late-night club. It serves lighter, refined takes on iconic French dishes.

name: Mandarin Food Store
cuisine: Hong Kong dai pai dong Cantonese
description: Mandarin Food Store is a casual Hong Kong-style Cantonese eatery styled like a classic dai pai dong food stall. It is known for traditional dishes such as braised beef brisket and stir-fried udon in generous, budget-friendly portions.

name: Mankeumi
description: Mankeumi is a compact Korean eatery serving homestyle meals in Melbourne's CBD. It is known for dishes like mandu dumplings, naengmyeon cold noodles and tteokbokki.

name: Market Lane Coffee
description: Market Lane Coffee is a Melbourne specialty coffee roaster and cafe group, known for fresh, in-season and traceable single-origin beans. It serves quality espresso and filter coffee across its city and market locations.

name: Maíz y Cacao
description: Maíz y Cacao is a Mexican restaurant and cafe celebrating Oaxacan cooking, with mole made from scratch. It serves traditional tamales and homestyle dishes alongside a Mexican-inflected brunch menu.

name: Mensho Tokyo
description: Mensho Tokyo is the first Australian outpost of Tomoharu Shono's acclaimed Japanese ramen chain. It's known for its creamy tori paitan (chicken) broth, duck char siu and inventive bowls like matcha and wagyu ramen, served walk-in only at a compact counter.

name: Miznon Melbourne
description: Miznon is a buzzing Israeli street-food eatery from chef Eyal Shani, set across two floors with an open kitchen downstairs and a quieter bar above. It's famous for its stuffed pitas built around fresh seasonal vegetables and its whole roasted cauliflower.

name: MoVida Aquí
description: MoVida Aquí is the spacious Bourke Street venue in the MoVida group, serving Spanish tapas, raciones and large paellas from a charcoal grill. It pairs the food with an extensive list of Spanish wines, beers and cocktails.

name: Mr Lee's Foods (Lee Seonsaeng)
description: Mr Lee's Foods is the CBD branch of a cult Korean-Australian restaurant with a deliberately tiny menu. It specialises in dwaeji-gukbap (milky pork-bone broth with rice and noodles in a hot stone bowl), house-made soondae and steamed pork belly.

name: Mr. Lee's Foods City
description: Mr Lee's Foods City is the CBD location of the beloved Korean comfort-food spot, focused on just three dishes: dwaeji-gukbap pork-bone soup, house-made soondae blood sausage and steamed pork belly. The milky broth arrives in a hot stone bowl with noodles, rice and kimchi.

name: Munch Bowl (川小碗)
description: Munch Bowl serves authentic Sichuan flavours in small, affordable single-serve bowls, letting diners mix and match dishes like mapo tofu and stir-fried Sichuan beef. It's a compact, fast-turnover spot with free rice refills.

name: My Lost Piece
description: My Lost Piece is a small laneway eatery serving traditional Taiwanese comfort food made fresh daily. It's known for crispy popcorn chicken, braised pork rice (lu rou fan), oyster pancakes and tofu pudding.

name: Nana Thai Style Hotpot & BBQ (Nana Mookata)
description: Nana Mookata serves mookata, a Thai-style hotpot where a domed barbecue grill sits in a moat of simmering soup. It specialises in fiery Isaan cuisine and stays open late, often with queues out the door.

name: No.1 Iron Pot Stew and Inn
description: No.1 Iron Pot Stew serves hearty Northeastern Chinese cuisine centred on bubbling iron-pot stews cooked tableside for sharing. Signature dishes include slow-stewed pork ribs, corn pancakes and guo bao rou.

name: Nora Thai
description: Nora Thai serves bold, fiery Southern Thai cuisine, with dishes like sour fish curry, pad sator (stir-fried stink beans), turmeric chicken soup and nam prik kapi shrimp-paste dip. The CBD venue is a larger second outpost of the South Yarra original.

name: Nori Maki
description: Nori Maki is an intimate Japanese counter-dining restaurant specialising in temaki handrolls made fresh to order in front of diners. It offers accessible omakase-style set menus focused on premium seafood and contemporary technique.

name: Oden
description: Oden is a Japanese dining and sake bar offering a refined take on oden, the simmered hot pot of fish cakes, vegetables and seafood in a light broth. It also serves premium sashimi, wagyu and seasonal a la carte dishes.

name: Ondo
description: Ondo is a Korean eatery serving approachable, lesser-known regional dishes alongside staples like bibimbap and dumpling soups. It is known for fine-dining-level cooking at casual prices and house drinks such as mugwort lattes.

name: Operator San
description: Operator San is a Japanese-inspired brunch cafe near Queen Victoria Market serving all-day breakfast and specialty coffee. Signature dishes include breakfast ramen, katsu curry Benedict and strawberry mochi waffles.

name: Operator25
description: Operator25 is a brunch cafe set in a heritage former telephone exchange, pairing Australian brunch favourites with Asian flavours. It serves Code Black specialty coffee and an owner-chef menu pitched at fine-dining level.

name: Osaka Bench
description: Osaka Bench is a tiny casual Japanese counter where dishes are cooked in front of you on a teppanyaki grill. It's known for okonomiyaki, omurice, ramen and bento.

name: Overlay Coffee
description: Overlay Coffee is a minimalist specialty cafe pouring quality espresso and cream lattes alongside a small selection of pastries. It's best known for its signature peanut butter cream latte.

name: Pa Tong Thai
description: Pa Tong Thai serves Southern Thai cooking with a focus on lesser-known Phuket dishes alongside familiar favourites. The room is decked out in colourful Sino-Portuguese style with cobalt walls and chandeliers.

name: ParkBongSook
description: ParkBongSook serves traditional Korean home-style meals built around rice, soup, kimchi and an array of banchan side dishes, all made in house. It offers authentic Korean dining in the CBD.

name: Pick Prik
description: Pick Prik serves Bangkok Chinatown-style Thai street food in a casual QR-order, pay-up-front space. It is known for somtum papaya salads, chargrilled moo ping skewers, tom yum seafood and Issan-style raw dishes.

name: Publique Bakery
description: Publique Bakery is a French-style boulangerie and patisserie opposite Queen Victoria Market, turning out sourdough loaves, croissants and Asian-influenced pastries. It is also known for very good espresso coffee and its signature bow-shaped Pain de Ruban filled with almond cream and raspberry.

name: Queen St. BB
description: Queen St. BB is an Italian-inspired dessert bar near Queen Victoria Market with a self-serve, pay-by-weight tiramisu counter offering around eight flavours. It also makes house gelato and espresso-based desserts in a vintage-styled space.

name: R.Harn
description: R.Harn is a Southern Thai restaurant opposite Melbourne Central, focusing on the lesser-seen dishes of southern Thailand with Malaysian influences. Highlights include stir-fried king prawns with pentai (stink) beans in house-made shrimp paste and the colourful kao yum rainbow salad.

name: Robata
description: Robata is a Japanese charcoal-grill restaurant built around a custom robatayaki hearth, from the San Telmo group. It is known for charcoal-grilled skewers, fresh sashimi and hearty mains in a neon-lit, Tokyo-inspired dining room.

name: Rongcha
description: Rongcha is a Southern Thai-style coffee shop serving kopi-style coffees, burnt butter toasts and Thai desserts alongside lunch sets like tom kha and seafood curries. It's known for its signature iced long black with coconut foam and caramelised toast range.

name: SOT Dining
description: SOT Dining is a contemporary Korean restaurant centred on sotbap, rice cooked in traditional steel pots and topped with your choice of protein, served through curated set menus. It takes a lighter, balanced approach to Korean shared dining with seasonal banchan and comforting soups rather than barbecue.

name: Sachi
description: Sachi is a Japanese seafood restaurant specialising in sushi, sashimi, chirashi don and omakase built around fresh and dry-aged fish. It offers a casual walk-in nigiri counter at lunch and a refined seasonal omakase in the evenings.

name: Sagye
description: Sagye is Melbourne's first dedicated gomtang house, serving slow-simmered Korean wagyu beef soup ladled over rice with house-made kimchi. The short menu pairs two variations of gomtang with pork mandoo, plus complimentary broth, rice and barley tea refills.

name: Sal's Authentic New York Pizza
cuisine: New York-style pizza by the slice
description: Sal's Authentic New York Pizza serves large, foldable New York-style pizzas available by the slice or whole pie. It's a casual CBD spot known for classic toppings like pepperoni and cheese.

name: Soi 38
description: Soi 38 is a cult Bangkok-style noodle and Thai street food spot tucked inside a CBD car park. It is known for boat noodles, papaya salad and authentic dishes served fast and cheap.

name: Stop Whining Seafood Wine Bar
description: Stop Whining is a tiny, casual oyster and seafood bar in Chinatown serving up to 16 varieties of fresh oysters. It pairs them with a curated wine and drinks list for a stay-a-while drink.

name: Taqueria Sin Nombre
description: Taqueria Sin Nombre is a hidden Chinatown laneway taqueria from the team behind Dingo Ate My Taco. It presses fresh masa tortillas on an imported machine for tacos like al pastor and birria, plus queso and other Mexican snacks.

name: Tian38
description: Tian38 is a contemporary Singaporean Chinese restaurant on Flinders Lane and an award-winning Chinese venue. It is known for wood-smoked roast duck, free-range Hainan chicken and dishes built for group dining.

name: Tomo's Little Collins
description: Tomo's Little Collins is a Japanese-style cafe known for quality coffee and hearty all-day breakfasts. It also runs Japanese pasta specials such as mentaiko carbonara udon on weekends.

name: Udon Yasan
description: Udon Yasan is a casual CBD noodle bar specialising in fresh, handmade Sanuki udon made onsite daily. It serves classic udon bowls alongside sides like teriyaki chicken.

name: Uncle Yoon
description: Uncle Yoon is a casual two-floor Korean-Chinese restaurant serving hearty dishes like jajangmyeon, tangsuyuk and jokbal hotpot. It's known for generous portions, fried chicken and cream rose tteokbokki at affordable prices.

name: Wang's Crispy Pancake
description: Wang's Crispy Pancake makes Jingzhou-style guokui, a crisp dough pocket from China's Hubei province filled with meat or vegetables and fired in a hot furnace. Fillings include spicy beef, pork and curry chicken, alongside sweet taiyaki.

name: Yamamoto Udon
description: Yamamoto Udon is a fast-casual noodle shop serving udon made fresh daily in small batches, including thick sanuki and ribbon-like himokawa styles across 18 varieties. Sides include karaage, tempura kakiage and tako wasabi.

name: Yaowarat
description: Yaowarat is a late-night Thai-Chinese eatery named after Bangkok's Chinatown road. It serves punchy street-food dishes, Chinese-style seafood and $1 congee in a lively, casual space.

name: Zabb Nua
description: Zabb Nua serves punchy, affordable Isan-style street food from Thailand's northeast, including som tam, crying tiger and Tom Yum noodles. Its signature dish is miang pla pao, a whole barramundi salt-baked and stuffed with lemongrass.

name: Hakka Cuisine
description: Hakka Cuisine is a Chinese restaurant specialising in traditional Hakka dishes with a modern twist, known for generous portions and signature plates like duck taro and Hakka pork balls. It also offers a dim sum menu and is BYO wine.

name: Afghan Kebab Carlton
description: Afghan Kebab Carlton is a fully halal Afghan restaurant serving char-grilled kebabs, mantu dumplings, qorma curries and platters. Favourites include the borani banjan eggplant and chicken qorma, with vegetarian options available.

name: Atsu
description: Atsu is a Japanese katsu specialist serving crisp tonkatsu, wagyu and chicken katsu over rice in donburi bowls, alongside kushikatsu skewers. Highballs are the focus of the drinks list, from classic lemon to nigori and tomato-miso riffs.

name: Attakai (Carlton)
description: Attakai is a cosy Japanese cafe serving whisked-to-order matcha sourced from Shizuoka and Uji, specialty coffee, and chef-made sandos such as wafu steak and tofu kinoko. It offers a relaxed full cafe experience with indoor and outdoor seating.

name: Cordelia
description: Cordelia is a sustainable seafood restaurant with a near-daily changing menu built around the day's catch and lesser-known fish species. Set on a sunny corner with a light, sea-inspired fit-out, it is a relaxed spot ideal for outdoor summer dining.

name: Hi Chong Qing (Hi CQ)
description: Hi Chong Qing is a casual noodle bar specialising in fiery, Sichuan-style Chongqing noodles. It's known for its signature spicy pork-bone and beef noodle bowls and is a popular, affordable spot praised among Melbourne's best.

name: Kantin - Indonesian Eatery
description: Kantin is a buzzing Indonesian eatery focused on the Manadonese cuisine of North Sulawesi, known for rich spices and bold sambals. Signature dishes include bakmi ayam rica, caramelised grilled beef ribs, and prawns in coconut woku gravy, all served halal.

name: Leonie Upstairs
description: Leonie Upstairs is an intimate Japanese sake and temaki sushi bar serving hand-rolled cones with fillings like otoro tuna belly, grilled eel and uni. It pairs a 50-strong sake list favouring female brewers with Japanese whisky, highballs and cocktails.

name: Mandina Kitchen
description: Mandina Kitchen is a Yemeni restaurant best known for mandi, slow-cooked smoky lamb or chicken over aromatic rice served with sahawiq. It brings traditional gulf-region flavours to Carlton in a relaxed neighbourhood setting.

name: Melitta Next Door
description: Melitta Next Door is a relaxed Mediterranean wine bar and eatery from the Bar Bellamy team. It serves dishes like lamb arrosticini, vegan skewers and slow-cooked stews alongside an Italian-leaning cocktail list and tap wines in a warm, colourful space.

name: Regale
description: Regale is a polished 150-seat bistro set inside the heritage Carlton Brewery building, serving Western dishes infused with bold Asian and Thai flavours. It is known for its lush booth seating, champagne service and a bar, Bar Galer, downstairs.

name: Torissong
description: Torissong is a contemporary Japanese eatery specialising in charcoal-grilled chicken yakitori skewers, sashimi and French-inspired clear-broth ramen. The intimate room centres on a 13-seat bar with charred vegetables and seafood rounding out the menu.

name: Waroeng SS (Spesial Sambal)
description: Waroeng SS (Spesial Sambal) is the first Australian outpost of a major Indonesian chain built around fiery house-made sambals. Diners pair around 20 styles of sambal with chicken, duck, beef or seafood and rice; the kitchen is halal with no alcohol served.

name: Yoru Yoru
description: Yoru Yoru is a Japanese parfait pop-up dessert bar by Kyoko Miyazaki, running out of the sake and temaki venue Leonie Upstairs. It serves layered Japanese-style parfaits in an intimate late-night setting.

name: Brico
description: Brico is a neighbourhood wine bar and bistro showcasing small producers and minimal-intervention wines from local and international makers. The produce-driven menu pairs classic wine-bar snacks like Sydney rock oysters and fresh baguette with cultured butter.

name: Edita's Fish 'N' Chips
cuisine: Fish & chips with Pacific Islander influence
description: Edita's is a family-run fish-and-chip shop putting a Pacific Islander spin on the Aussie classic. It's known for its viral fish burger, hand-cut chips and chop suey dim sims.

name: Malin
description: Malin is a one-hat modern French fine-dining restaurant set in a restored 1880s heritage building in Rathdowne Village. It serves an inventive, produce-driven menu in an intimate neighbourhood setting.

name: For Heaven's Steaks
description: For Heaven's Steaks is a Clayton sandwich shop famous for its hefty steak rolls made with halal-certified beef. It's a local favourite for some of the biggest, most loaded steak sandwiches in Melbourne's south-east.

name: HK Foodie (#HKFOODIE)
cuisine: Hong Kong cha chaan teng & street food
description: A Hong Kong-style cha chaan teng serving breakfast sets, macaroni soup, pineapple buns and street-food snacks like curry fish balls and tea eggs. Known for generous portions and budget-friendly prices, with dine-in, takeaway and delivery.

name: Taiwan Yes
description: A small Taiwanese eatery channelling night-market street food, with signature spicy fried pork belly and chicken rice bowls, popcorn chicken and braised minced pork rice. Tiny and casual with limited seating, it stays open late and is known for affordable, generous plates.

name: The 1985 Kafe
description: A cosy cafe specialising in Japanese-inspired desserts and fusion drinks, with vintage decor and visually striking creations like purple sweet potato tiramisu, yuzu chocolate mousse and strawberry matcha lattes.

name: CHAE
description: An intimate Korean fine-diner in the hills serving a seasonal degustation built around house-made jangs and traditional fermentation. Seating only a handful of guests per sitting, it showcases lesser-known Korean dishes prepared with time-honoured techniques.

name: Dua Bakehouse
description: A Scandinavian-inspired bakery with a Southeast Asian twist, known for its breads including shokupan, melonpan, pandesal and buttery shio pan rolls with savoury and sweet fillings. A slow-paced spot for pastries and loaves.

name: INI Coffee
description: INI Coffee is a minimalist all-white cafe pouring house blends and a rotating selection of single-origin filter coffees from Five Senses and Korea's Momos. Pastries from Sot by Mork and sandwiches from Via Porta round out the menu.

name: Lulu & Me Cheesecakes
description: Lulu & Me is a warehouse-style cheesecake shop known for its pay-by-weight, try-before-you-buy model. Flavours include Basque, pandan, Japanese and New York-style cheesecake, all made in the open kitchen on site.

name: N Lee Bakery
description: N Lee Bakery is one of Melbourne's earliest and best-loved banh mi shops, baking its own crusty rolls and house-made pate and mayo daily. Fillings range from barbecue pork and pork loaf to nem nuong and tofu, alongside Vietnamese iced coffee.

name: Pasta Classica
description: Pasta Classica is a hand-made fresh pasta shop and casual eatery serving daily-changing house-made pastas and sauces built on seasonal, locally sourced ingredients. Fresh pasta is also available to take home.

name: Zareh
description: Zareh is chef Tom Sarafian's Armenian and Lebanese restaurant centred on a custom wood oven and charcoal pit, with dishes such as harissa-spiced lamb cutlets and charcoal chicken kebab with toum. The drinks list leans into brandy and Lebanese arak.

name: Baker Bleu
description: Baker Bleu is a cult sourdough bakery serving naturally leavened bread, pastries and Market Lane coffee. Its Cremorne flagship adds an all-day menu of sandwiches, Roman-style pizzas, bagels and toasties.

name: Urumqi Uyghur Eatery
description: Urumqi Uyghur Eatery serves fully halal, authentic Uyghur cuisine in big, spice-laden portions. Specialties include hand-pulled noodles, lamb skewers, lamb dumplings and pilaf.
address: Shop 12, The Village, 81-125 Princes Hwy, Dandenong South

name: Kome Eatery
description: Kome Eatery serves home-style Japanese comfort food alongside specialty coffee in a calm, cozy space. The menu features onigiri, katsu sando, ochazuke, teishoku and Japanese curry rice.

name: Dawn Eatery
description: Dawn Eatery is a brunch cafe pairing Australian-style plating with Japanese and Korean flavours. It is known for dishes like the galbi sando, Korean fried chicken and a sakura latte.

name: El Columpio
description: El Columpio is a traditional Mexican spot sitting between a taqueria and a cenaduria, serving staunchly authentic, homestyle dishes. Expect tacos, pozole, tamales and chilaquiles, plus weekend barbacoa specials.

name: Frankie's Tortas and Tacos
description: A vibrant Mexican-inspired taqueria known for its tacos, hulking tortas (Mexican-style sandwiches) and house-made horchata. It also pours a deep range of rare tequilas and mezcals alongside imported beers.

name: Good Daze Canteen
description: A gelato shop scooping around 21 rotating flavours inspired by sweets from around the world, from Thai mango sticky rice to Aussie Milo crunch. A solid run of vegan options and lactose-free service round out the counter.

name: Lune Croissanterie
description: An institution devoted to the craft of the croissant, working out of a converted warehouse built around a climate-controlled glass pastry lab. Expect classic croissants, monthly specials and the seated Lune Lab experience.

name: MASTI Indian Restaurant
description: A modern Indian restaurant where every curry is made from scratch with freshly ground whole spices. The menu spans non-vegetarian, vegetarian and vegan dishes, backed by a notable range of premium Indian single malts.

name: Masses Bagels
description: A bagelry specialising in wild-fermented, slow-proofed bagels with creative seasonal toppings. Standouts include the everything bagel and hot-smoked trout, with vegan options available.

name: Obanzai Mama
description: Obanzai Mama is a Japanese deli serving traditional, largely vegetarian Kyoto-style obanzai home cooking. It is known for mix-and-match bento boxes of seasonal small dishes and a rotating selection of onigiri.

name: Rei
description: Rei is a Fitzroy izakaya serving modern Australian-Japanese small plates. It pairs Japanese bar food with sake and drinks in a casual setting.

name: St Andrews Hotel
description: St Andrews Hotel is a heritage Fitzroy pub serving classics like steaks, parma and fish and chips alongside seasonal gastropub dishes. The kitchen draws on British and Greek influences.

name: Elmo's Burgers
description: Elmo's is a Fitzroy North burger joint specialising in smash burgers with crispy-edged beef patties, American cheese and house sauce on brioche buns. It is regularly named among Melbourne's best smash burgers.

name: Lagotto
description: Lagotto is a bright Italian diner and bottle shop known for handmade pasta and focaccia. It pairs seasonal Italian dishes with a wine list focused on minimal-intervention and biodynamic drops.

name: Bánh Xèo Cô Ba
description: Bánh Xèo Cô Ba serves Da Nang-style banh xeo, with crispy pancakes that are thinner and lighter than the southern version, filled with pork, prawn and bean sprouts. Diners wrap them in rice paper with fresh herbs at the table, and the original Da Nang shop carries a Michelin Guide recognition.

name: Cô Thư Quán
description: Cô Thư Quán specialises in northern Vietnamese dishes that are rarely seen elsewhere in Melbourne, with rice paper salads and grilled meats among its signatures. The menu spans snacks, starters, salads, wraps, mains and vegetarian options.

name: First Taste
description: First Taste is a casual Cantonese restaurant known for its comforting congee, claypot rice and herbal soups. Standouts include the salted pork and century egg congee and the black pepper beef rice hot pot.

name: Kariton Sorbetes
description: Kariton Sorbetes is a Filipino-inspired ice-creamery whose gelato and sorbet draw on classic Filipino desserts and produce, including ube halaya (purple yam) and buko pandan (coconut and pandan). It's a small shopfront known for daily specials and queues out the door.

name: Matsu
description: Matsu is an intimate Japanese kaiseki restaurant serving a multi-course seasonal menu of roughly ten dishes, rounded out with tempura, rice, soups and dessert. It runs two nightly seatings Wednesday to Saturday for a small number of guests.

name: Xuan Anh Banh Cuon
description: A casual Footscray eatery specialising in banh cuon, delicate freshly steamed rice paper rolls filled with pork, chicken or shrimp. It is also known for hearty Northern Vietnamese noodle soups and handmade fish cakes.

name: Andzero
description: A cosy Japanese-inspired cafe in Glen Waverley serving teishoku breakfast sets, Wagyu hamburg and miso pork sandwiches on fluffy milk rolls, and specialty coffee. It also opens for dinner Tuesday to Saturday.

name: Gambino Restaurant & Rooftop
description: A two-level Italian dining venue in Glen Waverley pairing pizza and pasta with cocktails and a curated wine list. The upstairs rooftop turns into a lively weekend spot with DJs and city views.

name: Chef Wong Hawthorn East
cuisine: Cantonese yum cha & handmade dim sum
description: A casual yum cha restaurant serving handmade dim sum made by a chef with over 40 years of experience. Signature plates include har gow, siu mai and BBQ pork buns, ordered by the piece rather than from trolleys.

name: Daikokuten
description: A modern Japanese cafe in Hawthorn East known for its onigiri sets, Japanese curry and brioche-based brunch dishes. It also pours rotating micro-lot coffee alongside sesame, matcha and hojicha lattes.

name: The Bearded Pig
description: A pitmaster-run barbecue joint smoking brisket, pulled pork and burnt ends from a converted van. It opens only a couple of days a week with house-made sauces and regularly sells out.

name: Spicy Swan
description: A casual Sri Lankan eatery known for its flavourful curry combinations, kottu and godamba roti meals. Signature dishes include string hopper meals and a range of rolls and specialty curries.

name: Swatow Cafe
description: A small, busy restaurant serving authentic Teochew (Swatow) Chinese cooking. It is best known for braised duck, oyster omelette, congee and crispy shrimp rolls, with bookings recommended.

name: BaBa Chef
description: A homely Malaysian restaurant specialising in traditional street food. It is known for handmade fish balls and fish cakes, curry fish head and claypot fish ball soup.

name: Yaki Jin
description: An all-you-can-eat Japanese restaurant and bar serving izakaya dishes fresh to order for a fixed price. Tables get a personal grill for cooking gyukatsu, alongside sushi, wagyu and skewers.

name: Mietta by Rosemary
description: Mietta by Rosemary is an elegant boutique cake shop in Malvern crafted by pastry chef Rosemary Andrews. It is known for layered signature cakes including its Honey-Misu, an eight-layer carrot pecan cake and a two-texture chocolate cake.

name: Deli4You
description: Deli4You is a European-style Polish deli in Maribyrnong specialising in traditional smallgoods, cured meats and Eastern European groceries. It is known for its freshly made pierogi, pastries and continental cakes.

name: Marko's Schnitzel
description: Marko's Schnitzel is a casual takeaway shop in Moorabbin specialising in freshly crumbed schnitzel sandwiches, wraps and pita pockets. It is known for flavour-loaded options like the garlic, paprika and herb schnitzels, plus sides and breakfast rolls.

name: Bottari
description: Bottari is a Korean cafe beside Queen Victoria Market known for kkwabaegi, chewy twisted Korean doughnuts dusted with soybean powder. It also serves corn dogs, novelty doughnut lattes and Korean cafe drinks in a relaxed co-working-friendly space.

name: Manzé
description: Manzé is a small Mauritian restaurant and wine bar in North Melbourne with a seafood-leaning, charcoal-cooked menu of dishes like rougaille and masala-butter crab. It pairs the food with an extensive natural wine list and a broad range of rums.

name: ON.DOL Korean Cuisine
description: ON.DOL is a Korean barbecue restaurant in North Melbourne serving tabletop-grilled meats like wagyu and pork belly alongside pancakes, soups and cold noodles. It also offers an all-you-can-eat BBQ set.

name: Loku
description: Loku is a cosy Sri Lankan restaurant in Northcote serving home-style classics with inventive touches, from lamprais and pot biryani to a dozen curries and a signature betel leaf taco. The open kitchen lends an intimate, welcoming feel.

name: Nostra
description: Nostra is an Italian kitchen in Northcote built around the wood fire and the seasons, known for hand-rolled stuffed and saucy pastas plus ferments and produce-driven plates. It also runs a lively shared Sunday lunch series.

name: Onlys
description: Onlys is a neighbourhood wine and snack bar in Northcote pouring natural wine alongside simple, seasonal small plates. The intimate space, complete with fireplace, also hosts kitchen residencies and private events.

name: MEATing Korean BBQ Buffet Nunawading
description: MEATing is an all-you-can-eat Korean BBQ buffet in Nunawading, with premium meats including wagyu plus hot Korean dishes, street snacks and a DIY army stew station. It is family-friendly and a short walk from Nunawading station.

name: Kalimera Souvlaki Art
description: A souvlaki specialist serving handmade Greek street food with chargrilled lamb and chicken, hot chips and salads packed into pita. It's a destination spot in Oakleigh's Greek precinct, known for its traditional approach to the souvlaki.

name: King of the Roast
description: A casual takeaway shop famous for hearty roast meat rolls loaded with slow-cooked lamb, beef or pork and rich gravy on fresh bread. It's a no-frills local favourite for generous, well-priced roast sandwiches.

name: Migak
description: A Korean eatery offering more than 100 traditional and modern dishes cooked in a homestyle manner, with standouts like galbi tang and yangnyeom gejang. It's prized as a hidden gem for authentic flavours and take-home dishes.

name: MOJA Duck
description: A Chinese restaurant and bar specialising in Peking duck alongside a broad menu spanning live seafood and dishes from across China. It also serves as the dining room of the Parkmore Hotel, with private dining rooms available.

name: Hopper Joint
description: A lively Sri Lankan diner built around bowl-shaped hoppers and string hoppers served with coconut sambal and curries. Order the set for unlimited hoppers and a rotating choice of curries in a playful, communal setting.

name: Adana Sofra
description: Adana Sofra is a family-run Turkish charcoal grill in Reservoir specialising in shish kebabs, wraps and freshly made pide. Popular orders include the Adana wrap, mixed shish packs and house dips with ayran.

name: Burmese House
description: Burmese House is one of the few Melbourne restaurants dedicated to Burmese cuisine, serving family recipes in a rustic Richmond dining room. It is best known for its fermented pickled tea-leaf salad.

name: Cô Thư Quán
description: Cô Thư Quán serves Northern Vietnamese hawker-style street food in a nostalgic, vintage-decorated Richmond dining room. Signature dishes include Hanoi-style bun cha and soft-shell crab vermicelli.

name: Mushroom Kiosk
cuisine: Hong Kong cha chaan teng & cafe
description: Mushroom Kiosk is a Hong Kong-style cha chaan teng in Richmond serving traditional HK coffee and milk tea alongside cafe classics. Highlights include French toast with milk-tea syrup, mix-and-match combos and HK lemon tea.

name: Pacific Seafood BBQ House
description: Pacific Seafood BBQ House is a bustling Victoria Street institution known for Cantonese roast meats and fresh tank seafood. Diners come for Peking duck, char siu and salt-and-pepper calamari in a no-frills, BYO setting.

name: Warkop
description: Warkop is a snug Richmond cafe serving Indonesian-inspired sandwiches and coffee. It's known for kaya toast with coconut jam and rolls like chicken Taliwang and beef brisket with rendang sauce.

name: Attica
description: Attica is one of Melbourne's most acclaimed fine-dining restaurants, serving a seasonal multi-course tasting menu built around native Australian ingredients. Many of the herbs and produce are grown on the neighbouring Rippon Lea Estate.

name: Aoi Tsuki
description: Aoi Tsuki is an intimate 12-seat omakase bar where two chefs serve seasonal sushi degustations around a U-shaped counter. It holds Good Food Guide hats for its chef's-choice Japanese dining.

name: Emotional Oranges
description: Emotional Oranges is a South Yarra cafe specialising in delicate layered mille crepe cakes made with natural ingredients. Flavours range from matcha and jasmine tea to durian and strawberry brulee.

name: France-Soir
description: France-Soir is a long-running South Yarra brasserie serving classic French bistro fare with theatrical, French-speaking service. It's known for its traditional menu and an extensive wine list of Australian, New Zealand and French bottlings.

name: Steer Dining Room
description: Steer Dining Room is a New York-style steakhouse specialising in premium Australian and Japanese Wagyu, with more than 30 cuts including Kobe A5. It pairs the grill with an extensive wine list of over 500 selections and tableside service.

name: Warung Coffee & Sandwiches
description: Warung Coffee is an Indonesian-influenced cafe serving specialty coffee alongside toasties and sandwiches such as the Indomie toastie and beef rendang sandwich. It also pours matcha and houjicha lattes for dine-in and takeaway.

name: itteki 一滴 Matcha + Cafe
description: itteki is a Japanese-style cafe focused on ceremonial-grade matcha alongside specialty coffee and a menu of light cafe fare. It is known for its matcha drinks and minimalist tea-house feel.

name: Kolkata Cricket Club
description: Kolkata Cricket Club is a modern Indian restaurant at Crown serving regional and Bengali dishes, known for its butter chicken and charcoal-tandoor naan and meats. It spans a casual front bar and a white-tablecloth dining room inspired by India's historic members clubs.

name: Bun Bun Bakery Springvale
description: Bun Bun Bakery is a Vietnamese bakery widely regarded as serving some of Melbourne's best banh mi, with crusty house-baked rolls and generous fillings like roast pork, lemongrass chicken and BBQ pork. It is a busy takeaway-only spot with no seating.

name: Martabak Pecenongan 78
description: An outpost of the famous Indonesian chain serving martabak — thick stuffed pancakes in both sweet versions (chocolate, condensed milk, cheese, nuts) and savoury egg-and-meat fillings. The Springvale kitchen runs alongside Rojo Sambel, rounding out the menu with noodles and other Indonesian dishes.

name: Rojo Sambel & Martabak Pecenongan 78
description: A halal Indonesian eatery built around fiery sambal, serving spiced rice plates, noodles and traditional comfort dishes. It shares its Springvale premises with Martabak Pecenongan 78, so sweet and savoury stuffed pancakes are on offer too.

name: Cô Thư Quán St Albans
description: An authentic St Albans Vietnamese restaurant specialising in South-Central regional cooking. It is known for pho, bún bò Huế, bánh xèo and com tam, with vegetarian options on the menu.

name: Garam Merica
description: A compact Indonesian counter-serve specialising in warteg-style nasi bungkus — banana-leaf rice parcels you build from a rotating spread of spiced meats and vegetables. The selection changes every few weeks to showcase dishes from across Indonesia's regions.
address: Ground Floor, 348 St Kilda Rd, Melbourne

name: Pizzicotto Pizzeria e Cucina
description: A neighbourhood Italian restaurant in Strathmore turning out wood-fired pizzas and homemade pasta alongside antipasti, focaccia and salads. Rustic regional cooking with vegan, vegetarian and gluten-free options available.

name: Café Sunshine & Salamatea
cuisine: Persian cafe by day, banquets by night
description: Persian cafe and restaurant serving breakfast staples by day and shared banquets at night. Run as a social enterprise that trains and employs refugees and asylum seekers.

name: Federal Taung Zalat
description: Casual Burmese restaurant tucked inside Sunshine Plaza's food court. Known for hearty home-style dishes such as goat curry, mohinga and spicy anchovies.

name: Kababi
description: Persian charcoal grill serving smoky kababs cooked over hot coals alongside comforting home-style dishes. Signature plates include saffron sumac lamb and chicken kababs with barberry rice.

name: Xuan Banh Cuon
description: Family-run Northern Vietnamese spot famous for banh cuon, delicate steamed rice rolls filled with pork and prawn. Also serves grilled pork wraps and northern-style noodle soups.

name: Yuki Tei Tempura Bar
description: Intimate tempura bar where the chef fries each piece to order in an omakase format at dinner. Lunch offers a tighter menu of donburi, curry and gyoza.

name: 279 | Coffee & Musubi
description: A relaxed corner café serving rotating specialty coffee roasts alongside Japanese musubi (rice parcels) with an Australian twist. Known for fillings like bacon-and-egg and tempura prawn, plus pour-over coffee.

name: Chàm
description: A contemporary Vietnamese café overlooking Queen Victoria Market, blending traditional dishes with Melbourne brunch sensibilities. Known for its sizzling banh mi chao skillet, egg coffee and banana latte.

name: JUSIK
description: An authentic Korean restaurant specialising in tabletop barbecue and Korean fried chicken, with a range of traditional sides. Bibimbap and set menus are popular among its largely Korean clientele.

name: Myth Cafe
description: A counter-service café serving authentic Malaysian Hakka and hawker-style dishes for breakfast and lunch. Known for yong tau foo, herb-laden khao jam and kaya butter toast with soft-boiled eggs.

name: Sanhe Congee
description: A specialist dedicated to Chinese rice porridge, offering around 30 savoury congee varieties built on a closely guarded umami seasoning blend. Also serves dim sum staples like char siu bao, shrimp dumplings and fried turnip cake.

name: The Flour Melbourne
description: The Flour is a cosy West Melbourne patisserie turning out refined European-style pastries, canelés, cakes and cookies with seasonal Australian touches. It's known for its Mont Blanc and an award-winning single-origin coffee program.

name: Warung Agus
description: Warung Agus is a long-running, family-run Balinese restaurant serving authentic dishes such as babi guling (roast pork) and betutu, plus a wide range of vegan and gluten-free options. The intimate dining rooms and parklet seating bring a touch of Bali to West Melbourne.

name: Na He Bistro
description: Na He Bistro brings the flavours of China's Yunnan province to Windsor, with a menu drawing on the region's ethnic-minority cooking and vibrant fresh ingredients. It serves dinner nightly from early evening.

name: Coracle
description: Coracle is a bright, minimalist Yarraville cafe serving Asian-influenced breakfast and lunch dishes inspired by Vietnamese and Hong Kong flavours, alongside seasonal lunch sets. Drinks include Vietnamese coffee, imported Japanese matcha and hojicha, and espresso.
